text:
Once upon a time in the quiet village of Hawthornleath, there lived a peculiar old owl named Oliver. Oliver had no feathers that touched the ground; instead, he wore a cloak of dark, glossy moss.

Each morning, Oliver would soar above the whispering trees, watching the villagers go about their daily lives. They spoke largely in whispers around him. The villagers were curious about Oliver. Some came closer out of curiosity, while others just admired his grand old eyes that twinkled in the moonlight.

Oliver had a secret. Every year, on the Fourth of July, he’d invite a small gathering under the great oak near the river to recount the adventures of his life. Tales of faraway lands, magical creatures, and times that seemed like dreams filled the air.

One particular year, a particularly daring fox had boasted it could climb the Whispering Willow, which reached sky-high into the mountains. The villagers knew this was silly talk — the willow was ancient and thick.
